Chronology of every recorded fact about [[Indirial]], in reading order. Generated from the same verified fact store as the main article.

== House of Blades ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 11''' — Indirial is someone who would kill Kai and Denner if they tried anything.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 23''' — Indirial is the oldest confidant and most trusted servant of Leah's father.}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 1''' — Indirial is a character who wears a black Nye cloak.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 1''' — Indirial has a daughter named Elaina.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 1''' — Indirial's parents were cut apart by Damascan soldiers.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 1''' — Indirial wears a Nye cloak and has chains on his arms.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 1''' — Indirial possesses a Dragon’s Fang named Vasha.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 1''' — Indirial can make his sword vanish.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 1''' — Indirial possesses a diamond that provides clarity and simplicity.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 1''' — Indirial believes the Enosh Travelers' success in destroying the Tree would make no one safe.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 3''' — Indirial is an Overlord who has been a fixture around the royal palace.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 3''' — Indirial is an Overlord and a Valinhall Traveler.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 3''' — Indirial has black chain tattoos on his arms.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 3''' — Black chains crawl up Indirial's forearms.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 4''' — Indirial once saved Kai by dragging him to the healing pool.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 4''' — Indirial is someone Kai intends to alert.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 4''' — Indirial fought Valin and stood with the Damascans.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 4''' — Indirial is a character who sprints through the halls of Cana's royal palace.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 5''' — Indirial provided information to Leah about Simon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 8''' — Indirial might be able to lure the Incarnation back into his home Territory.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 9''' — Indirial is a person associated with Kathrin and the old master.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 9''' — Indirial is a Valinhall Traveler with dark hair and dark skin who has black chain-marks on his arms.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 9''' — Indirial is an Overlord of the realm of Cana and bearer of the first Dragon's Fang.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 9''' — Indirial uses a chipped and pitted Dragon's Fang.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 10''' — Simon had previously watched a battle involving Indirial.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 12''' — Simon is stalling until Indirial's reinforcements arrive.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 15''' — Indirial possesses a medallion with a tiny dark gem at its center.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 15''' — Indirial is the son of Aleias.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 15''' — Indirial is the Overlord of the realm of Cana.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 15''' — Indirial is a subject to the authority of His Majesty Zakareth the Sixth.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 15''' — Indirial is the son of Kalman.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 15''' — Indirial is an Overlord.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 15''' — Indirial offered Leah sympathy when her mother died.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 16''' — Indirial has been missing.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 17''' — Indirial assured Simon that Naraka is the fastest and most reliable way to Travel between set points.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 18''' — Indirial challenged Valin after seeing through his insanity.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 18''' — Indirial fought Kai.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 18''' — Indirial worked for the King.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 20''' — Indirial is a person who handles emergencies and puts out fires around the camp.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 21''' — Leah signaled Indirial for help.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 21''' — Indirial commands the Tartarus group.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 21''' — Indirial is a Valinhall Traveler.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 21''' — Indirial cleared his throat.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 23''' — Indirial held a small box for Leah.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 25''' — Indirial is someone Simon hopes will be home.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 25''' — Indirial was talking strategy with a captain named Erastes.}}

== City of Light ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 2''' — Zakareth wants Indirial on his side.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 3''' — Indirial's forearms are marked with shadowy chains.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 4''' — Indirial's left hand is covered by a Valinhall gatecrawler, which is a spiked black gauntlet.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 6''' — Indirial is a person Valin reminds Simon of.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 6''' — Indirial said he needed Simon's help.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 6''' — Indirial previously saved Simon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 7''' — Indirial is an Overlord with black chain marks on his forearms.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 7''' — Indirial was hired by Leah's father to guard the ruling monarch.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 7''' — Indirial uses a long, cracked sword.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 7''' — Indirial has the ability to cut open a Valinhall Gate.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 9''' — Indirial spent better than an hour inside Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 9''' — Indirial uses an unorthodox application of a lesser-known Valinhall power.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 10''' — Indirial finished a discussion with Feiora.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 11''' — Indirial shouted warnings to people to get back.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 11''' — Indirial is a Valinhall Traveler and the son of Aleias.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 11''' — Indirial is part of the group taking out Alin's army.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 12''' — Indirial has a grip like an Ornheim golem.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 12''' — Indirial can wear a plate of translucent green light like armor.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 13''' — Indirial was found sprawled on the steps of a waystation.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 13''' — Indirial is an Overlord who can call a flickering version of ghost armor.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 14''' — Indirial is attempting to cut a Gate.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 14''' — Indirial is near death.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 14''' — Lycus wants to help Indirial.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 16''' — Indirial is a person the Eldest considered for the Nye essence instead of Simon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 16''' — Indirial would likely bring everything to his Queen first.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 17''' — Indirial was suspended in a prison of black ice.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 17''' — Indirial uses liquid steel from Valinhall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 17''' — Zakareth tells Indirial that if he does not survive, others will not either.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 17''' — Indirial called stone.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 18''' — Indirial is a voice of caution in Leah's mind.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 19''' — Indirial's wife and daughter have also been denied water.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 19''' — Indirial can call upon steel to keep himself on his feet.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 20''' — Indirial wields a straight sword in his left hand and a Dragon's Fang in his right.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 20''' — Indirial's focus is filtered by the Valinhall diamond.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 20''' — Indirial possesses a weapon named Vasha.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 20''' — Indirial's presence hangs over others like a banner.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 20''' — Indirial has succumbed to his chains.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 20''' — Indirial is a powerful avatar of the House wandering in the outside world.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 21''' — Indirial was last seen in the camp five days ago.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 21''' — Indirial stands next to King Zakareth with chains on his arms turned to steel and a cloak of shadows.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 21''' — Indirial is a warrior and a trusted servant, more than just an Incarnation.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 21''' — Indirial represents the Incarnation of Ragnarus.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 22''' — Indirial felt Kai's rage and the destruction of Tartarus.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 22''' — Indirial is the Valinhall Incarnation Kai encountered on the tiles of the House of Blades.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 22''' — Indirial is the first student of Kai.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 22''' — Leah suspects the King may have taken Indirial.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 23''' — Indirial is an Incarnation.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 23''' — Indirial has the ability to summon weapons from the Valinhall armory.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 23''' — Indirial possesses Nye essence.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 23''' — Indirial possesses a Territory.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 23''' — Indirial is the Valinhall Incarnation.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 24''' — Indirial is a shadow-cloaked Incarnation sitting in the entry hall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 24''' — Indirial has violet eyes.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 24''' — Indirial has been sealed into the Territory.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 25''' — Indirial became an Incarnation after fighting another Valinhall Traveler.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 25''' — Indirial has been corrupted.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 25''' — Indirial attacked the House of Blades and was sealed in the House.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 26''' — Indirial was forced to embrace Valinhall and become an Incarnation.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 27''' — Leah has Indirial with her.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 27''' — Zakareth mentions Indirial's job.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 28''' — Indirial oversees the day-to-day management of his realm from within Valinhall.}}

[[Category:Timelines]]
